name: Contrast Framing
summary: A persuasion technique that clarifies value by comparing the old way and the new way in a sharp, meaningful way.
whenToUseIt: Use contrast framing when buyers are still underestimating the difference between staying the same and changing, especially in demos, business cases, and executive conversations.
steps: Describe the current-state approach honestly and clearly.
Show what the improved state changes in outcome, speed, control, or risk.
Make the difference feel practical rather than abstract.
example: You contrast spreadsheet-based pipeline reviews that take hours and still feel uncertain with a live inspection view that lets managers challenge risk in real time.
